title: Icy Herby Cornish Rest-meat Hot Pot
description: A comforting British dish featuring a flaky pastry crust, herbal flavors, and a tamasic-friendly rest-meat filling, served in a hot pot vessel.
instructions: Roll out the shortcrust pastry to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Place the pastry in a 9x13 inch (23x33 cm) hot pot vessel.
Sauté the garlic, parsley, and sage in olive oil until fragrant.
Add the leftover rest-meat and cook until lightly browned.
Add the potatoes, lettuce, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Pour in the white wine vinegar and stir to deglaze the pan.
Cut the pastry crust to fit the top of the hot pot vessel and crimp the edges to seal.
Brush the pastry with a little bit of olive oil and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake the hot pot in the preheated oven for 30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is hot and bubbly.
